title: Sparganium dolichocarpum
taxonomy: Sparganium dolichocarpum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Typhaceae, commonly known as the cattail family. It belongs to the genus Sparganium, which includes several species of bur-reeds. The species is native to North America and is characterized by its elongated, green fruiting spikes. Sparganium dolichocarpum is a perennial aquatic plant that grows in shallow water and is often found in wetlands and marshes. It is also known as the long-fruited bur-reed due to its distinctive fruiting structure.

intro: Sparganium dolichocarpum, also known as tall bur-reed, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the family Typhaceae, characterized by its tall, slender stems and umbrella-like inflorescences. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues, including bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Sparganium dolichocarpum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sparganium dolichocarpum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as in the form of tinctures and syrups, to take advantage of its medicinal benefits.
